description: Manage skills, agents, prompts, instructions, hooks, and related AI coding resources
config.instructionFile.description: Optional file to sync a generated instruction block
config.instructionFile.markdownDescripti:
**Instruction Sync Target** - Optional file to receive the generated instruction block. With `coexistenceMode = auto`, Resource NINJA writes the shared `agent-ninja` block for the current scope; `independent` keeps the legacy `resource-ninja` skill block. Workspace targets index workspace resources. Global Resource Home targets such as `~/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md` index Global Resource Home resources. Installed files stay in their native paths; the generated block is an index, not a copy.

| File | Tool |
|------|------|
| none | Disable instruction file sync |
| AGENTS.md | GitHub Copilot, Copilot CLI, Claude Code |
| ~/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md | GitHub Copilot CLI global local instructions |
| .github/copilot-instructions.md | GitHub Copilot repository instructions |
| .github/instructions/SkillList.instructions.md | GitHub Copilot path-specific instructions |
| CLAUDE.md | Claude Code |
| .claude/CLAUDE.md | Claude Code / VS Code Claude compatibility |
| .claude/CLAUDE.local.md | Claude local-only instructions |
| .cursor/rules/skills.mdc | Cursor |
| .windsurfrules | Windsurf |
| .clinerules | Cline |
config.instructionFile.agents: AGENTS.md (GitHub Copilot, Copilot CLI, Claude Code)
config.customInstructionPath.markdownDes: Custom file path for the generated instruction block *(only used when Instruction File is set to 'custom')*
config.resourcesDirectory.description: Default directory for workspace skill resources
config.resourcesDirectory.markdownDescri: **Workspace Skill Directory** - Default workspace install root for `skill` resources and skill metadata. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.workspaceAgentsDirectory.markdown: **Workspace Agent Directory** - Default workspace install root for `.agent.md` resources. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.workspaceInstructionsDirectory.ma: **Workspace Instruction Directory** - Default workspace install root for `.instructions.md` resources. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.workspacePromptsDirectory.markdow: **Workspace Prompt Directory** - Default workspace install root for `.prompt.md` resources. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.workspaceHooksDirectory.markdownD: **Workspace Hooks Directory** - Default workspace root for hook resources. Copilot CLI hook config files such as `.github/hooks/policy.json` are detected directly; packaged hook resources can still use `<directory>/<hook>/README.md`. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.workspaceMcpDirectory.markdownDes: **Workspace MCP Directory** - Staging and review directory for MCP config JSON resources. Files are copied here first; during install you can keep them for review, discard them later, or explicitly merge compatible servers into `.vscode/mcp.json`. Existing server keys require confirmation before overwrite. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.userAgentsDirectory.markdownDescr: **User Agent Directory override** - Optional install and scan root for User Profile agents. Leave empty to store `.agent.md` files in the VS Code User `prompts` folder, which is discoverable by the agent picker. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.userInstructionsDirectory.markdow: **User Instruction Directory override** - Optional install and scan root for User Profile instructions. Leave empty to use the VS Code User `instructions` folder. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.userPromptsDirectory.markdownDesc: **User Prompt Directory override** - Optional install and scan root for User Profile prompts. Leave empty to use the VS Code User `prompts` folder. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.globalResourceHomePreset.markdown: **Global Resource Home Preset** - Choose the shared resource root shown in User / Global Resource Home and used by the Global install target. The default is `~/.copilot` for GitHub Copilot CLI resources (`copilot-instructions.md`, `skills/`, `agents/`, `hooks/*.json`, `mcp-config.json`). Claude-compatible and open-agent roots are also available. The selected preset is used only when `#resourceNinja.globalHomeDirectory#` is empty.
config.globalResourceHomePreset.custom: Custom - use Global Resource Home Directory override
config.globalHomeDirectory.markdownDescr: **Global Resource Home Directory override** - Optional custom shared resource root. Any non-empty value overrides the selected `#resourceNinja.globalResourceHomePreset#`; leave empty to use the preset. The default preset is `~/.copilot`. Resource Ninja scans product-native files at the root (for example `copilot-instructions.md` and `mcp-config.json`) plus kind-specific subfolders such as `skills/`, `agents/`, `instructions/`, `prompts/`, and `hooks/`. Copilot CLI runtime logs and session state are skipped. Accepts workspace-relative, absolute, and `~/` paths.
config.coexistenceMode.auto: Use Single Block + Owner Handoff when the skill-only sibling extension is also active
config.coexistenceMode.independent: Keep Resource NINJA markers independent (legacy parallel-block behavior)
config.coexistenceMode.markdownDescripti: **Coexistence Mode** - `auto` uses the shared `agent-ninja` marker block with owner handoff. `independent` keeps the legacy Resource NINJA block for advanced troubleshooting.
config.kindsExcluded.markdownDescription: **Excluded Resource Kinds (legacy compatibility)** - Deprecated compatibility list of resource kinds to omit from the shared instruction block when Resource NINJA is the only active owner. The new instruction-block policy settings control the default kinds. Legacy exclusions are ignored while the skill-only sibling extension is active, and `skill` is always kept.
config.instructionBlock.includeAgents.ma: **Instruction Block: Add Agents** - Add `agent` resources to generated instruction blocks such as AGENTS.md for workspace targets. Default: off. `skill` entries are always included; prompts, hooks, MCP configs, plugins, and Cursor rules stay in their native views.
config.instructionBlock.includeInstructi: **Instruction Block: Add Instructions** - Add `instruction` resources to generated instruction blocks such as AGENTS.md for workspace targets. Default: off. `skill` entries are always included; prompts, hooks, MCP configs, plugins, and Cursor rules stay in their native views.
config.instructionBlock.globalHome.inclu: **Global Resource Home Instruction Block: Add Agents** - Override the workspace agent listing policy for Global Resource Home instruction targets. `inherit` reuses `#resourceNinja.instructionBlock.includeAgents#` so you do not need to enter the same choice twice.

**Global Resource Home Instruction Block: Add Instructions** - Override the workspace instruction listing policy for Global Resource Home instruction targets. `inherit` reuses `#resourceNinja.instructionBlock.includeInstructions#` so you do not need to enter the same choice twice.
config.instructionBlock.globalHome.on: Force on for Global Resource Home targets
config.instructionBlock.globalHome.off: Force off for Global Resource Home targets
config.useSharedSourcesManifest.markdown: **Shared Sources Manifest** - Read and write the cross-extension `sources.json` manifest under the shared Agent NINJA directory so both extensions can reuse the same source list.
config.useSharedResourceIndex.markdownDe: **Shared Resource Index** - Read and write the cross-extension `index.json` resource cache under the shared Agent NINJA directory. Falls back to the local cache if the shared store is unavailable.
config.autoUpdateInstruction.description: Automatically update instruction file on install/uninstall
config.autoUpdateInstruction.markdownDes: **Automatically update the instruction sync target** after resource install/uninstall and local skill registration. With `coexistenceMode = auto`, Resource NINJA refreshes the shared `agent-ninja` block for workspace or Global Resource Home resources. In `independent` mode, it refreshes the legacy `resource-ninja` skill block. Output-related setting changes still regenerate managed output immediately even when this setting is off. Disable this only if you want resource changes to stop updating the managed output automatically.
config.autoUpdateResourcesOnUpgrade.mark:
**Update installed remote skills** when extension is upgraded

- `always`: Automatically reinstall remote skills
- `prompt`: Ask before updating
- `never`: Do not update
config.includeLocalResources.description: Include fallback-discovered local skill files in the instruction file
config.includeLocalResources.markdownDes: When enabled, include workspace-wide fallback `SKILL.md` files outside the configured workspace skill directory in the generated instruction block *(requires Auto Update Instruction enabled)*. Workspace Resources still scans configured directories first.
config.githubToken.description: GitHub Personal Access Token for API requests (increases rate limit from 60 to 5000 requests/hour)
config.githubToken.markdownDescription: GitHub Personal Access Token (increases rate limit from 60 to 5000 requests/hour). For public resources, no repository scopes are required; leave scopes unchecked unless you intentionally index private repositories. [Create a token](https://github.com/settings/tokens/new?description=Agent%20Resources%20Ninja)
config.useRefOutput.markdownDescription: **Use Ref Output** - Toggle whether generated instruction blocks stay lightweight by linking to per-kind catalogs. Keep this on for always-loaded files such as `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `copilot-instructions.md`. Turn it off when you want the instruction block itself to render the table directly.
config.outputFormat.markdownDescription: **Output Format** - Choose the inline layout used in the generated instruction block when **Use Ref Output** is off. `full` keeps the IMPORTANT prompt plus the detailed table, `compact` keeps the IMPORTANT prompt plus a shorter index, and `legacy` keeps the compatibility table only.
config.outputFormat.full: Full - IMPORTANT prompt + detailed table (200 chars)
config.outputFormat.compact: Compact - IMPORTANT prompt + compressed index (100 chars)
config.refCatalogFormat.full: Full index - source, repository, remote URL, and long descriptions
config.refCatalogFormat.compact: Compact index - path plus shorter descriptions
config.refCatalogFormat.legacy: Legacy index - simple resource table only
config.refCatalogFormat.markdownDescript: **Ref Index Detail Format** - Choose how detailed the generated README indexes are when **Use Ref Output** is on. Each resource kind writes to its native README location such as `.github/skills/README.md`, `.github/agents/README.md`, or `~/.copilot/prompts/README.md`. `full` keeps source, repository, remote URL, and long descriptions; `compact` keeps path plus shorter descriptions; `legacy` keeps a simple resource/description table without the IMPORTANT prompt.
config.singleClickInstall.description: Install remote resources with a single click
config.singleClickInstall.markdownDescri: Install remote resources with a single click in Remote Resources. When disabled, double-click installs instead. Click and double-click installs use **Default Install Target**; the context menu command still asks for a target and offers Custom.
config.defaultInstallTarget.markdownDesc:
**Default Install Target** for single-click and double-click installs from Remote Resources.

- **Workspace**: resource kinds use the configured Workspace directories. MCP config resources are copied under the Workspace MCP Directory first, then the install flow lets you keep them for review or explicitly merge compatible servers into `.vscode/mcp.json`.
- **User Profile**: agents and prompts use the VS Code User `prompts` folder by default; instructions use the VS Code User `instructions` folder; skills/hooks/MCP config resources use the selected Global Resource Home.
- **Global Resource Home**: all resource kinds go under the selected Global Resource Home preset or override (`~/.copilot` by default, or `~/.claude`, `~/.agents`, or a custom path).
- **Ask every time**: show the install target picker.

The context menu **Install Resource** command always asks and also supports Custom.
config.defaultInstallTarget.ask: Ask every time - show the install target picker
config.defaultInstallTarget.userData: User Profile - install into VS Code User paths or Global Resource Home for skills/hooks
config.defaultInstallTarget.globalHome: Global Resource Home - install into the selected shared resource root
config.showBuiltInResources.markdownDesc: Show VS Code, GitHub Copilot Chat, and GitHub Copilot CLI built-in resources in User / Global Resource Home. Your own files under the selected Global Resource Home are always shown; this toggle only controls whether product-shipped built-in resources are shown in source-specific groups. Built-in resources are visible by default.
config.remoteResourceViewMode.markdownDe: Choose how Remote Resources are grouped. Repository-first is best for browsing by source; resource-type-first is best when looking for a specific kind such as prompts or hooks.
config.remoteResourceViewMode.repository: Repository first - group by repository, then resource type
config.remoteResourceViewMode.resourceTy: Resource type first - group by resource type, then repository

Browse bundled and GitHub resource sources here.
